  ```text
    "mergePayloads": 2,
    "mirrorTextPreservedInProcess": "DO-NOT-SEND-PR-91192-PROOF-TEXT",
    "mediaPayloadText": null,
    "mediaTrusted": true,
    "mediaAudioAsVoice": true,
    "outboundTexts": [],
    "telegramResult": {
      "messageId": "3515",
      "chatId": "<redacted-private-group>"
    }
  }

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  6 passed (6)

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  217 passed (217)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 25.83s

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  48 passed (48)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 30.77s
  ```
- Observed result after fix: the real Microsoft TTS provider generated local speech media, the PR-head merge helper split the source-reply transcript mirror from the trusted media payload, the media payload had `mediaPayloadText: null`, the Telegram adapter received one media send with `asVoice: true` and empty `text`, and the live Telegram API accepted it as `operation=sendVoice` / `deliveryKind=voice` with message id `3515`. The proof harness recorded `outboundTexts: []`, so the private source/final text did not go to the room.
- What was not tested: this contributor environment did not restart or deploy the live OpenClaw Gateway on the PR branch, so this is not a full multi-process inbound Telegram/Discord agent turn running from production Gateway. The live proof exercises real configured TTS generation, the patched PR-head merge/media payload shape, the PR-head Telegram outbound adapter, and actual Telegram voice-note transport. The focused dispatch regressions cover the suppressed block/final delivery branches in-process.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the Telegram chat/topic is private, so the public PR body redacts chat id and thread id. A maintainer/Mantis run inside a maintainer-controlled Telegram Desktop session would still be the strongest public-facing visual proof, but the live send above proves the voice-note transport path without a Gateway restart.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper re-review on `a069d38f11b` found that pending trusted media-only `onBlockReply` payloads could still be dropped before the media check, that the test config used unsupported TTS mode `block`, and that live after-fix transport proof was still needed. This patch adds regressions for the code findings and the proof above shows live Telegram `sendVoice` on the current PR head.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec tsx --eval <merge-helper proof script>` — passed; real temp local media split into trusted media-only payload.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — passed, 2 shards, 223 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` — passed, 1 shard, 48 tests.
- `pnpm check:test-types` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts` — passed.
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts` — passed.
- `pnpm check:changed` — not rerun in this pass; earlier local Crabbox delegation failed before code checks with `selected binary failed basic --version/--help sanity checks`.

## Notes

This keeps the scope narrow: trusted local media gets a deliverable media-only path for merge, block, and final routes, while ordinary source replies and untrusted media stay suppressed under `message_tool_only`.

## Current review state

Author patch for ClawSweeper's code findings is pushed at `0c5b76a444e77cf28062acd78ba04c5342aa11c6`. ClawSweeper's latest code review cleared the P1 implementation findings with patch quality `🐚 platinum hermit`; the remaining blocker was live transport proof. This body now includes redacted live Telegram `sendVoice` proof from the current PR head plus the focused dispatch regressions. Next action: rerun the real-behavior gate and request ClawSweeper re-review.

target.title: feat(tui,gateway): show CLI-routing label in /model picker
target.body:
## Summary

The `/model` picker today shows the human-readable model name as the description line (e.g. `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6  Claude Opus 4.6`). When a model is pinned to a CLI backend via user-config `agents.defaults.models[...].agentRuntime.id` (e.g. `claude-cli`, `google-antigravity-cli`), the user has no immediate visual cue that the model will be routed through a local CLI rather than the direct API.

This PR adds an optional `runtimeLabel` field to `TuiModelChoice` and the gateway `ModelChoiceSchema`, and shows it in the picker as `"CLI"` whenever the user has pinned a CLI runtime. The runtime label takes precedence over the model name; falls back to the existing display name otherwise.

## Scope

Display-only — never used as a routing key. Symmetrical between both list-models sources:

- `src/agents/model-selection-cli.ts` — new helper `resolveModelRuntimeLabel(provider, modelId, cfg)` next to the existing `isCliProvider`.
- `src/tui/embedded-backend.ts` — `listModels()` populates `runtimeLabel` for embedded mode.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.ts` — `buildPublicModelsListEntry` populates `runtimeLabel` for the gateway RPC.
- `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agents-models-skills.ts` — `ModelChoiceSchema` gains `runtimeLabel: Type.Optional(NonEmptyString)`.
- `src/tui/tui-backend.ts` — `TuiModelChoice` gains `runtimeLabel?: string`.
- `src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts` — picker description prefers `runtimeLabel` over `name`.

## Deliberately Out Of Scope

- 3-way classification (`CLI`/`OAuth`/`API`). MVP only emits `"CLI"`. OAuth-vs-API requires inspecting per-agent auth-profiles and is left to a follow-up PR.
- Statusline format (`runtime-id/model-id` → `provider/model-id`). Separate root cause — `sessionInfo.modelProvider` carries the runtime id; needs deeper investigation.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Picker did not show whether a model is routed through a CLI backend; the description line repeated the human-readable name.

**Real environment tested:** macOS, source repo `/Users/rob/Development/openclaw`, branch `feature/display-runtime-labels`. User config `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` has `anthropic/claude-*` pinned to `agentRuntime.id = "claude-cli"` and `google/gemini-*` pinned to `agentRuntime.id = "google-antigravity-cli"`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
pnpm exec vitest run \
  src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts \
  src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts \
  src/agents/model-selection-cli.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.models-voicewake-misc.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output, real run, no mocks beyond the new unit-test fixture:

```
Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  114 passed (114)
```

`pnpm tsgo:core` exit=0, `pnpm tsgo:extensions` exit=0.

**Observed result after fix:** Picker description column now shows `"CLI"` for any model whose user-config `agentRuntime.id` resolves to a CLI backend via `isCliProvider(...)`. Models without a pinned CLI runtime continue to show the human-readable name as before.

**What was not tested:**

- Live `/model` picker in a running TUI session against this source build (deferred to user smoke after merge or via `pnpm gateway:dev`).
- 3-way OAuth/API classification (out of scope, see above).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: Project channel activity into gateway health snapshots
target.body:
Fixes openclaw/openclaw#90985.

## Summary

Project recorded channel activity timestamps into gateway health snapshots so the health/status surface reflects known inbound and outbound activity.

- **Fresh snapshots** fill `lastInboundAt` and `lastOutboundAt` from the channel activity tracker when plugin/runtime snapshots do not already provide them. An existing runtime-sourced timestamp is never overwritten.
- **Cached health responses** merge recorded channel activity before returning. Newer cached timestamps are preserved — no backdating.
- The original cached object is never mutated; the merge works on a clone.

This does not send messages, probe channels, or change runtime channel behavior. It only updates the health/status surface.

## Changed files

| File | What changed |
|---|---|
| `src/commands/health.ts` | `projectRecordedChannelActivity` helper, called in `getHealthSnapshot` |
| `src/gateway/server-methods/health.ts` | `mergeCachedAccountActivity` / `mergeCachedChannelActivity` helpers, called in `mergeCachedHealthRuntimeState` |
| `src/commands/health.test.ts` | New `getHealthSnapshot` describe block |
| `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts` | New cached health activity merge test |

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `getHealthSnapshot` and the cached health merge path did not project recorded channel activity timestamps into health responses, while the sibling `getStatusSummary` in `src/commands/status.js` already did. This meant `lastInboundAt` and `lastOutboundAt` were missing from gateway health output even when the channel activity tracker had recorded values.

**Real environment tested:** Windows 11, Node 24.15.0, openclaw repository at commit `a4f8de6945` on branch `fix/gateway-health-channel-activity`. I do not have a live OpenClaw gateway running locally. The behavior gap was confirmed by source inspection.

**Exact steps or command run after fix:**

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/health.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts --maxWorkers 1 --testTimeout 15000 --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
✓ getHealthSnapshot › projects recorded channel activity onto fresh health snapshots  20ms
✓ gateway healthHandlers.health cache freshness › merges recorded channel activity into cached health responses without backdating  1ms

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       140 passed (140)
```

The tests exercise the real `recordChannelActivity` / `getChannelActivity` logic directly — those helpers are not mocked. The assertions verify concrete timestamp values flowing through the patched code paths.

**Observed result after fix:** Fresh health snapshots correctly fill missing `lastOutboundAt` from the activity tracker while preserving an existing runtime `lastInboundAt: 500`. Cached health merge correctly picks the newer of recorded vs cached outbound timestamp (`900 > 100`) and does not backdate a newer cached inbound (`500 > 250`). The original cached object is confirmed unmutated after merge.

A maintainer with a running gateway can verify live with:

```
pnpm openclaw health --json
```

Look for `lastInboundAt` and `lastOutboundAt` populated in channel entries after any channel activity is recorded.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway JSON output — no running OpenClaw instance available locally. Discord plugin accounting, provider/auth paths, fallback behavior, Windows portability fixes (kept on a separate branch).

## Out of scope

- Discord plugin accounting changes
- Runtime patch files
- Provider / auth changes
- Fallback behavior
- Semantic memory changes
- Gateway service / always-on changes
- Windows portability fixes (separate branch)

## AI-assisted

Built with OpenAI Codex. I reviewed and understand the changes. The fix mirrors the existing pattern in `src/commands/status.js` which already projects channel activity onto the channels status surface.

target.title: fix(cron): add Authorization header to provider preflight probe
target.body:
## Summary

Replaced the hand-rolled Authorization header logic in the cron preflight probe with the shared `resolveProviderRequestHeaders()` from `provider-request-config.ts`. This ensures the preflight and normal model request paths build identical auth headers for the same config.

## Changes

### P1: Reuse shared auth normalization (ClawSweeper finding)

- **model-preflight.runtime.ts**: Replaced the manual `if (params.apiKey) { headers["Authorization"] = ... }` / custom-header construction with delegation to `resolveProviderRequestHeaders()` via `sanitizeConfiguredProviderRequest()`
- Auth is now resolved through the same code path as normal model requests — any future changes to auth formatting apply automatically
- Full credential chain preserved: `request.auth` override → `resolveApiKeyForProvider` fallback → no auth

### P0: Error redaction at cache-write time

- **model-preflight.runtime.ts**: Applied `formatErrorMessage()` to errors before storing in `preflightCache`, preventing unredacted error objects from remaining in heap between cache write and read

### P0: Header prefix trimming consistency

- Prefix is now trimmed via shared `sanitizeConfiguredProviderRequest`, matching the behavior of `resolveAuthOverride` in the normal request path

### P2: Cross-path auth header parity tests

- **model-preflight.runtime.test.ts**: Added 4 parity tests (P0) verifying preflight and normal path produce identical auth headers for the same config
- Added 4 edge case tests (P1): empty header fields, unknown mode, whitespace token, OAuth marker filtering
- Total: 31 tests pass (was 23)

## Files Changed

| File | Δ |
|------|---|
| `src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.ts` | +99/-40 |
| `src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.test.ts` | +413/-3 |

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Cron job preflight was building custom auth headers (`request.auth.mode === "header"`) with hand-rolled logic that missed prefix trimming, header collision cleanup, and `request.headers` merging — inconsistent with the shared auth normalization used by normal model requests.

**Real environment tested:**
- **OS:** Windows 11
- **Runtime:** Node.js v24.14.0
- **Branch:** fix/cron-preflight-clean at d0f8be2fdf

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
pnpm vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.test.ts
pnpm vitest run src/agents/provider-request-config.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output captured after running the tests:
```
 ✓ src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.test.ts (31 tests) 564ms
 ✓ src/agents/provider-request-config.test.ts (30 tests) 62ms
All 61 tests pass across both files. No regressions.
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 31 preflight tests pass, all 30 provider-request-config regression tests pass. Code review confirmed: no Critical or Important issues, auth delegation is correct with proper fallback semantics and edge case handling. Test engineer confirms: thorough coverage at correct mock boundaries.

**What was not tested:**
- Integration test with actual local provider endpoint (preflight tests mock `fetchWithSsrFGuard`)
- `prepareProviderRuntimeAuth` plugin auth exchange path (documented gap — preflight intentionally skips this for local provider probes)

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`, and unify the cron source-delivery planner into a single shared helper used by both the normal execution path and the version-skew fallback.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. **Unified planner**: Extracted `resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` as the single canonical cron source-delivery planner in `source-delivery-fallback.ts`. Both the normal cron execution path (`run.ts`) and the version-skew fallback path (`run-executor.ts`) now call this same helper — eliminating the duplicate implementation.
2. The helper calls `resolveCronDeliveryPlan(job)` to determine the delivery mode from the job config, then delegates to `createSourceDeliveryPlan` with parameters matching **current main's semantics**:
   - **webhook**: `owner: "none"`, message tool **disabled**, no direct fallback
   - **none**: `owner: "none"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, no direct fallback
   - **announce**: `owner: "direct_fallback"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, `directDelivery: true`, `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from resolved delivery status
3. `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` is a thin wrapper that derives the `CronDeliveryPlan` from the job and calls the shared helper
4. **Removed all legacy field sniffing** — no `toolPolicy`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, or `messageChannel` reads from stale callers. The fallback derives everything from the job's delivery config, matching how current main resolves it.
5. Added `CronSourceDeliveryResolvedTarget` type for the shared contract
6. Added `logWarn` diagnostic when fallback activates

This ensures there is exactly one cron source-delivery planning code path, eliminating future drift between normal and fallback semantics.

## Tests

20 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:

**`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` unit tests (4 tests):**
1. `delivery.mode "none"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_none"`, message tool enabled but not forced, no direct fallback
2. `delivery.mode "announce"` → `owner: "direct_fallback"`, `reason: "cron_announce"`, direct fallback enabled, `skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from `resolvedDelivery.ok`
3. `delivery.mode "webhook"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_webhook"`, message tool **disabled**, empty target
4. Isolated agentTurn with no delivery config → defaults to announce behavior

**`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` parity tests (4 tests):**
5. `none` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
6. `announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
7. `webhook` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
8. `implicit announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper

**`createCronPromptExecutor` level (4 tests):**
9. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "none"` → falls back with `messageToolForced: false`, `owner: "none"`
10. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "announce"` → `direct_fallback` semantics, `forceMessageTool: false`
11. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "webhook"` → message tool disabled
12. No delivery config on isolated agentTurn → announce behavior fallback

**`executeCronRun` level (4 tests):**
13. Stale legacy params (`sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy.forceMessageTool`) → **ignored**, uses job delivery config instead
14. Valid `sourceDelivery` → pass-through, no fallback (regression)
15. Webhook mode → `disableMessageTool: true`, `forceMessageTool: false`
16. Announce mode → `direct_fallback` defaults, `disableMessageTool: false`, `forceMessageTool: false`

## Real behavior proof (current head)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Issue #85051 — isolated cron executor crashed with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')` when a version-skew/stale caller invoked the executor without a `sourceDelivery` argument. This branch guards that path via the shared planner `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` (reconstructing a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` from job delivery config + `resolvedDelivery`), and preserves duplicate-announce suppression: in announce mode, when `resolvedDelivery.ok` is missing/undefined it defaults to `true` so `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` stays on (no double-post).

- **Real environment tested**: Checkout commit `8c684df354ac9cff91e0197d29787e3ed9534b31` (branch `fix/cron-isolated-source-delivery-guard`). Primary clean-room run inside Docker `node:24-bookworm` (Node `v24.16.0`, Linux `aarch64`, fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` inside the container with host `node_modules` shadowed by an anonymous volume so Linux-native binaries are used). Cross-checked on host: macOS 15 (Darwin 24.3.0) arm64, Node `v24.10.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`. Harness runs `tsx` against the LIVE `src/` source (no mocks of the planner).

- **Failing proof before fix**: The pre-guard upstream/main shape is the raw dereference at `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts:182` on `upstream/main`:

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts | sed -n '182p'
    const sourceReplyDeliveryMode = params.sourceDelivery.sourceReplyDeliveryMode;
  ```

  `upstream/main` has no `source-delivery-fallback.ts` (the guard does not exist there):

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts
  fatal: path 'src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts' exists on disk, but not in 'upstream/main'
  ```

  The harness reproduces that exact dereference against a stale-caller params object that omits `sourceDelivery`. It throws the issue's exact error (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   A. STALE-CALLER CRASH (pre-guard upstream/main dereference)
  ============================================================
  thrown: true
  error.name: TypeError
  error.message: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')
  PASS: pre-guard deref throws TypeError on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: error message matches "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')"
  ```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  ```
  # Docker (primary, clean room)
  docker run --rm -v "$PWD":/repo -v /repo/node_modules -w /repo \
    -e NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node:24-bookworm bash -lc '
      corepack enable
      corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts'

  # Host (cross-check)
  node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:

  Guarded reconstruction for `announce` / `none` / `webhook` with `sourceDelivery` OMITTED — no crash, valid plans rebuilt (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   B. GUARDED reconstruction (this branch, sourceDelivery OMITTED)
  ============================================================
  announce plan: {
    "owner": "direct_fallback",
    "reason": "cron_announce",
    "target": { "channel": "messagechat", "to": "room-1" },
    "normalFinal": "visible",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": true, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": true },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": true, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": true, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: announce: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: announce: owner === direct_fallback
  PASS: announce: reason === cron_announce
  PASS: announce: fallback.directDelivery === true
  PASS: announce: target rebuilt from resolvedDelivery
  none plan: {
    "owner": "none",
    "reason": "cron_none",
    "target": { "channel": "messagechat", "to": "room-1", "accountId": "acct-1", "threadId": "thread-1" },
    "normalFinal": "private",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": true, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": true },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": false, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": false, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: none: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: none: owner === none
  PASS: none: reason === cron_none
  PASS: none: messageTool.enabled === true
  PASS: none: messageTool.force === false
  PASS: none: fallback.directDelivery === false
  webhook plan: {
    "owner": "none",
    "reason": "cron_webhook",
    "target": {},
    "normalFinal": "private",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": false, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": false },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": false, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": false, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: webhook: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: webhook: owner === none
  PASS: webhook: reason === cron_webhook
  PASS: webhook: messageTool.enabled === false
  PASS: webhook: target === {}
  ```

  Duplicate-suppression behavior across `resolvedDelivery.ok` variants, plus normal-path/fallback parity:

  ```
  ============================================================
   C. DUP-SUPPRESSION (announce; resolvedDelivery.ok variants)
  ============================================================
  ok=MISSING -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok MISSING -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true (suppression preserved)
  ok=false   -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: false
  PASS: ok=false -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === false
  ok=true    -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok=true -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true
  PASS: normal-path planner matches fallback wrapper when ok missing

  ============================================================
   RESULT: 22 passed, 0 failed
  ============================================================
  ```

  Existing regression suites (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  === vitest: source-delivery-guard ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts (17 tests) 15ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  17 passed (17)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.41s

  === vitest: double-announce ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts (68 tests) 218ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  68 passed (68)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.96s
  ```

  Host cross-check (macOS arm64, Node v24.10.0) — same results: harness `22 passed, 0 failed`; vitest `17 passed (17)` and `68 passed (68)`.

- **Observed result after fix**: With `sourceDelivery` omitted (the version-skew stale-caller path), the pre-guard upstream deref throws the exact `TypeError` from issue #85051, while this branch's `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` reconstructs a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` for every delivery mode (`announce` → `direct_fallback`/`cron_announce`, `none` → `none`/`cron_none`, `webhook` → `none`/`cron_webhook`) without crashing. Duplicate-announce suppression is preserved precisely as designed: missing `resolvedDelivery.ok` → `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true` (suppression on, so a message-tool send already delivered to the target does NOT trigger a second direct-fallback post); `ok:false` → `false`; `ok:true` → `true`. The normal-path planner (`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run.ts`) and the fallback wrapper (`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run-executor.ts`) produce identical plans for the missing-`ok` case, confirming both seams share one policy.

- **What was not tested**: No live Telegram/Discord/transport round-trip was exercised; there is no real message bus in this harness. The proof exercises the planner + executor source-delivery boundary directly at the LIVE source level (the function whose contract the guard protects), plus the colocated Vitest suites that cover the executor wiring (`createCronPromptExecutor` / `executeCronRun`) and the double-announce timer fallback. End-to-end delivery dispatch through an actual channel adapter, and the actual cross-version skew between a stale compiled caller and the new executor in a deployed gateway, were not reproduced.


Fixes #85051

commands.author: nocodet666

target.title: fix(parallel): bound MCP success response body read
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`postMcp` in `parallel-mcp-search.runtime.ts` reads the HTTP response body in a
single ternary expression — but only the **error** branch is bounded:

```ts
text: response.ok
  ? await response.text()                                                   // ← unbounded
  : await readResponseTextLimited(response, PARALLEL_MCP_ERROR_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES),  // ← 8 KiB cap
```

The asymmetry is visible on adjacent lines in the same ternary: `readResponseTextLimited`
is already imported for the error path, yet `response.text()` is used on the
success path with no size guard. A misbehaving or hostile Parallel AI MCP
endpoint can stream an arbitrarily large JSON-RPC body into memory on the
success path, causing OOM — while the identical code one line below already
knows to cap the error body.

This follows the same campaign as Alix-007's `#95103`/`#95218` response-limit
fixes, but applied to the MCP transport layer rather than provider JSON
endpoints.

## Changes

* `extensions/parallel/src/parallel-mcp-search.runtime.ts`:
  - Adds constant `PARALLEL_MCP_SUCCESS_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES = 16 * 1024 * 1024`
    (16 MiB — same default as `readProviderJsonResponse`; matches expected
    MCP tool-call payload sizes while guarding against runaway bodies).
  - Replaces `await response.text()` in the success branch of `postMcp` with
    `await readResponseTextLimited(response, PARALLEL_MCP_SUCCESS_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES)`.
  - No new imports — `readResponseTextLimited` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`
    is already present for the error branch in the same expression.
  - No test changes needed — test file already uses real `Response` objects
    and all 17 tests pass unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

* **Behavior addressed:** A Parallel AI MCP endpoint can no longer stream an
  arbitrarily large success body into memory; the success-branch read is now
  capped at 16 MiB, matching the error branch's capped pattern, and the stream
  is cancelled on overflow.
* **Real environment tested:** A real `node:http` `createServer` bound to
  `127.0.0.1`, streaming a 24 MiB body in 64 KiB chunks with **no
  Content-Length** header, driving the real exported `readResponseTextLimited`
  helper on Node v22.22.0.
* **Exact steps:**
  1. Boot server; `GET /huge-success` (200) and `GET /huge-error` (400) stream
     24 MiB each; `GET /small` returns a valid MCP JSON-RPC response.
  2. Drive `readResponseTextLimited(res, 16 MiB)` against `/huge-success` and
     assert text length ≤ 16 MiB and bytes-on-wire stayed near cap.
  3. Drive `readResponseTextLimited(res, 8 KiB)` against `/huge-error` and
     assert text length ≤ 8 KiB and bytes-on-wire stayed near cap.
  4. **Negative control**: run OLD `await response.text()` against
     `/huge-success` and measure total bytes.
  5. Drive against `/small` and assert the JSON-RPC payload is returned intact.
* **Evidence after fix:**
  * Success cap: text length **16,777,216 bytes** (16 MiB); server sent
    **18,677,760 bytes** — near cap, not the full 24 MiB body.

    **589,824 bytes** — near cap, not the full 24 MiB body.
  * Negative control: unbounded `response.text()` pulled the **full
    25,165,824 bytes**, proving both caps are load-bearing.

* **Observed result:** `ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED`.

  `"bounds initialize error bodies without using response.text()"` regression.
* **What was not tested:** Live Parallel AI MCP endpoint calls. The proof
  measures bytes-on-wire and text truncation, which is the load-bearing signal.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: perf: avoid event-loop stall during embedded_run bootstrap-context
target.body:
## Summary

Fix event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context that block the event loop for 14-22 seconds, causing message loss.

### Root causes addressed

1. **Synchronous file I/O** — `readFileSync`/`closeSync` in workspace file loading blocks the event loop while reading root bootstrap files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, etc.)
2. **Unbounded blocking glob walks** — `fs.glob` for extra bootstrap files traverses the entire workspace tree synchronously inside Node, including `.git`/`node_modules` directories, without yielding to the event loop
3. **Uninterrupted hook chains** — `triggerInternalHook` executes all handlers back-to-back without yielding

### Changes

**`src/agents/workspace.ts`**
- Replace sync `readFileSync`/`closeSync` with callback-based async `readFdToString`/`closeFdAsync`
- Parallelize root bootstrap file reads via `Promise.all` (previously serial `for` loop)
- Replace `fs.glob` with an async traversal walker that yields via `setImmediate` every 256 entries, so a sparse pattern like `**/AGENTS.md` over a large workspace never blocks the event loop. The walker performs no directory pruning — it walks the full tree and returns every file matching the configured patterns, with no traversal cutoff and no artificial match cap; the downstream bootstrap character budget owns content limiting.
- Suppress `closeFdAsync` rejections to avoid masking read errors on NFS/FUSE

**`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`**
- Add `yieldBetweenHandlers` option with `setImmediate` yield between hook handlers
- Add per-handler timing instrumentation with handler index reporting
- Log warnings for slow handlers exceeding 500ms threshold

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**
- Instrument bootstrap-context substages (`workspace-file-load`, `hook-overrides`) with `performance.now` timing
- Log substage durations when total bootstrap-context exceeds 2 seconds
- Enable `yieldBetweenHandlers` for `agent:bootstrap` hook triggers

**`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`** / **`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts`**
- Support new async workspace file loading and timing callback interfaces

### Tests

- `workspace.test.ts` — 3 new tests for async fd read (happy path, cache hit, fd close on failure)
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — every matching file returned without an artificial cap (no directory pruning), and a regression test asserting a late-appearing match in a large tree is still loaded (no traversal cutoff)
- `handler.test.ts` — no truncation warning is emitted for large glob scans
- `internal-hooks.test.ts` — 3 new tests for yield-between-handlers ordering, handler timing reporting, and slow handler warning

All existing tests pass without modification.

### Performance impact

- Root bootstrap file loading: serial sync → parallel async (estimated 3-5x speedup for 8 files)
- Glob walks: synchronous/blocking → async with periodic `setImmediate` yields (prevents multi-second event-loop stalls on large workspaces). The walk completes fully; all configured matches are kept, and the existing bootstrap character-budget truncation handles content limiting.
- Hook chains: uninterrupted → yielded (prevents monopolizing the event loop during bootstrap)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context loading caused by synchronous I/O and a blocking `fs.glob` walk over large workspaces, which blocks the loop and drops messages. This change makes the glob walk async/yielding so the loop stays responsive, while preserving every configured `bootstrap-extra-files` glob match (the earlier iteration of this branch added a hard 20,000-entry traversal cutoff that silently dropped late matches — that cutoff is removed here).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, run via `tsx` against the real exported `loadExtraBootstrapFiles` from `src/agents/workspace.ts`. Before/after captured from two git commits on the PR branch: before = `5849e709f4` (walker with the 20,000-entry cutoff), after = `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed).
- **Real environment scenario**: A workspace of ~26,100 filesystem entries (a 3-tier tree, 30×30×28 filler files) with a single configured-glob target `AGENTS.md` planted in the subtree the depth-first walker drains last, so the target is only reached after more than 20,000 visited entries. A 5ms repeating timer runs during the load as an event-loop liveness probe (steady ticks ⇒ the loop never stalled).
- **Failing proof before fix**: commit `5849e709f4` (cutoff present):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 82.9ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 16 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 0
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: false
  RESULT: late configured glob match was DROPPED (pre-fix behavior)
  ```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # against the patched checkout (commit 5ce395d645) a script that builds the
  # ~26,100-entry workspace, starts a 5ms liveness timer, then calls the real
  # loadExtraBootstrapFiles(root, ["**/AGENTS.md"]) and checks the deep match:
  npx tsx proof-live.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from `node`/`tsx` against commit `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 99.4ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 20 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 1
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: true
  RESULT: configured glob match beyond old cutoff is PRESERVED; event loop stayed live
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the cutoff removed, the configured `**/AGENTS.md` match located past the old 20,000-entry frontier is now loaded (1 file returned vs 0 before in the terminal output above), so existing broad `bootstrap-extra-files` configs no longer silently lose AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md context. The liveness timer kept ticking throughout the async walk (20 ticks during the ~99ms load), confirming the periodic `setImmediate` yields — not the cutoff — are what keep the event loop responsive.
- **What was not tested**: No full live `embedded_run` with active channel connections was driven end-to-end; this proof exercises the real glob-resolution code path and event-loop liveness directly rather than through a connected channel. The focused regression coverage lives in `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` and `handler.test.ts`.

Closes #87509

commands.author: nocodet666

target.title: fix(volcengine): bound TTS success response text reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/volcengine/tts.ts` reads its TTS success response bodies with
unbounded `await response.text()` on two paths:

* `seedSpeechTTS` — BytePlus Seed Speech: reads line-delimited JSON frames
  where each frame carries a `data` field with base64-encoded audio.
* `legacyVolcengineTTS` — legacy Volcengine TTS: reads a single JSON body
  carrying the full base64-encoded audio payload.

Both bodies carry base64-encoded PCM/MP3/OGG audio data, which can legitimately
reach several MiB per request. Neither path has any size cap today. A
misbehaving or hostile Volcengine/BytePlus TTS endpoint (including a
self-hosted `baseUrl` override) can stream an arbitrarily large body into memory
before parsing, causing OOM.

This closes the same unbounded-body OOM class targeted by the
`#95103`/`#95108`/`#95218` response-limit campaign, now applied to the
Volcengine TTS success paths.

## Changes

* `extensions/volcengine/tts.ts`:
  - Adds `import { readResponseTextLimited } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http"`.
  - Adds constant `VOLCENGINE_TTS_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES = 16 * 1024 * 1024` (16 MiB —
    same default cap used for JSON responses by `readProviderJsonResponse`).
  - Replaces `await response.text()` in `seedSpeechTTS` with
    `readResponseTextLimited(response, VOLCENGINE_TTS_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES)`.
  - Replaces `await response.text()` in `legacyVolcengineTTS` with
    `readResponseTextLimited(response, VOLCENGINE_TTS_BODY_LIMIT_BYTES)`.
  - No test file exists for these functions; no test changes needed.

## Real behavior proof

* **Behavior addressed:** A misbehaving Volcengine/BytePlus TTS endpoint can no
  longer stream an arbitrarily large body into memory; both TTS success-body
  reads are now capped at 16 MiB, and the stream is cancelled on overflow.
* **Real environment tested:** A real `node:http` `createServer` bound to
  `127.0.0.1`, streaming a 24 MiB body in 64 KiB chunks with **no
  Content-Length** header, driving the real exported `readResponseTextLimited`
  helper (the same function added to `tts.ts`) on Node v22.22.0.
* **Exact steps:**
  1. Boot server; `GET /huge` streams 24 MiB with no `Content-Length`;
     `GET /small` returns a valid single TTS JSON frame
     (`{"code":0,"data":"<base64>"}`).
  2. Drive `readResponseTextLimited(response, 16 MiB)` against `/huge` and
     assert the returned text length ≤ 16 MiB and bytes-on-wire stayed near cap.
  3. **Negative control**: run the OLD `await response.text()` against `/huge`
     and measure total bytes pushed by server.
  4. Drive against `/small` and assert the frame is returned intact.
* **Evidence after fix:**
  * Bounded case: returned text length **16,777,216 bytes** (exactly 16 MiB cap);
    server sent **17,694,720 bytes** — near the cap, not the full 24 MiB body.
  * Negative control: the unbounded `response.text()` forced the server to push
    the **full 25,165,824 bytes** (~1.5× the bounded read), proving the cap is
    load-bearing.
  * Small case: frame text returned intact with `"code":0` present — no loss.
* **Observed result:** `ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED`. Bounded-reader enforcement
  is covered by the existing `readResponseTextLimited` unit tests in
  `packages/media-core`.
* **What was not tested:** Live Volcengine/BytePlus TTS API calls. The proof
  instead measures bytes-on-wire and text truncation, which is the load-bearing
  signal.

## Evidence

```
[case 1] oversized TTS body, cap=16 MiB, NO content-length
  ok: readResponseTextLimited truncated at cap (got 16777216 bytes) — true
  ok: bytes on wire stayed near cap, not full body (sent 17694720) — true

[negative control] OLD unbounded response.text()
  ok: unbounded read pulled the FULL ~24 MiB body (sent 25165824) — true

[case 3] small valid TTS frame reads intact
  ok: small frame text returned — true
  ok: small frame content intact — true

ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED
```

Proof script: `scripts/proof-volcengine-tts-bound.mjs`
Node version: v22.22.0

**Label**: security

AI-assisted.

target.title: fix(chutes-oauth): bound core helper JSON response reads at 16 MiB
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`src/agents/chutes-oauth.ts` had 3 unbounded `await response.json()` calls (userinfo fetch, token exchange, token refresh). A hostile Chutes endpoint, proxy, or CDN can return an oversized HTTP 200 JSON body with no `Content-Length` and stream an arbitrarily large payload — `response.json()` buffers everything into memory before parsing, making this an **OOM vector** on a path that runs with user-attached OAuth credentials.

This is the JSON bounded-read analogue for the deprecated core Chutes helper (the active plugin path at `extensions/chutes/oauth.ts` is covered by sibling PR #96249).

## Changes

- `src/agents/chutes-oauth.ts:119,159,230` — replace 3 `(await response.json()) as Type` calls with `readProviderJsonResponse<Type>(response, "<label>")`. Labels: `"Chutes userinfo"`, `"Chutes token exchange"`, `"Chutes token refresh"`. (+6/-5)

## Real behavior proof

Drives production `readProviderJsonResponse` over a real `node:http` server (127.0.0.1, chunked transfer, no Content-Length). Covers AFTER (capped), BEFORE (unbounded OOM vector), and negative control. Node v22.22.0, Linux x86_64.

```
=== AFTER (16 MiB cap via readProviderJsonResponse) ===

  PASS  hostile oversized body: overflow error thrown
  PASS  valid token exchange response: parsed correctly
  PASS  valid userinfo response: parsed correctly

=== BEFORE (unbounded response.json() — OOM vector) ===

  unbounded read consumed 32.0 MiB — OOM vector confirmed
  PASS  (negative control) unbounded read exceeded 16 MiB cap

=== All chutes-oauth bounded-read assertions: 4/4 passed ===
```

- **Evidence after fix**: 4/4 assertions pass. AFTER: hostile 32 MiB body triggers bounded overflow error, valid token/userinfo responses parse correctly. BEFORE: unbounded read consumes 32.0 MiB (OOM vector confirmed).
- **Behavior addressed**: 3 unbounded `response.json()` calls → capped at 16 MiB via shared SDK helper.
- **Real environment tested**: real `node:http` server (127.0.0.1, chunked transfer, no Content-Length) driving production `readProviderJsonResponse`. Node v22.22.0.
- **Negative control**: unbounded read consumed 32.0 MiB — proves the cap is load-bearing.
- **What was not tested**: live Chutes API call (the proof exercises the same production SDK helper against the same attack shape); cross-platform Node differences.

## Out of scope

- `extensions/chutes/oauth.ts` (active plugin path) — covered by sibling PR #96249.
- Other unbounded `response.json()` call sites in core — covered by bounded-read campaign PRs (#96776, #96772, #96782, etc.).

## Risk

- **Low**: swap to `readProviderJsonResponse` — same error propagation shape, 16 MiB default cap invisible to normal responses (OAuth token responses < 1 KiB). Existing tests: 6/6 pass unchanged.

Sibling PR: #96723 bounds anthropic provider SSE streaming read. #96249 bounds the Chutes OAuth plugin path. Together these close the bounded-read gap for the Anthropic + Chutes OAuth surfaces.

AI-assisted: implemented and proof-tested with AI assistance; reviewed by a human before submission.

commands.author: SunnyShu0925

target.title: fix(agents): enable bundled static catalog fallback for cron Attempt 2
target.body:
## Summary

Plugin-provided models (e.g. opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash) in the bundled static catalog are now resolvable by non-plugin-harness cron sessions via the embedded runner's Attempt 2 path.
- **Problem**: Non-plugin-harness isolated cron sessions fail with "Unknown model" for plugin-provided models, even though the models exist in the bundled static catalog. The embedded runner's two-attempt model resolution in `run.ts:930-984` — Attempt 1 (skipAgentDiscovery) necessarily fails, and Attempt 2 (after `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`) calls `resolveModelAsync` without `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`, so the static catalog is never queried.
- **Solution**: Pass `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback: true` in the Attempt 2 `resolveModelAsync` call, enabling the static catalog fallback path for plugin-provided models.
- **What changed**: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts` (+5 lines)
- **What did NOT change**: Attempt 1 behavior (still skipAgentDiscovery); Attempt 1 static catalog fallback for pluginHarnessOwnsTransport; model discovery/registry behavior; configured fallback surface; error handling

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #95500
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

The embedded runner's model resolution for non-plugin-harness cron sessions uses a two-attempt strategy (run.ts:930-984). Attempt 1 sets `skipAgentDiscovery=true` (intentionally skips agent discovery for performance). When Attempt 1 fails, `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` runs and Attempt 2 retries without `skipAgentDiscovery`. However, Attempt 2 did not set `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`, so plugin-provided models registered in the static catalog (like `opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash`) were never queried through the static catalog fallback path. This caused legitimate plugin provider models to fail with "Unknown model" in isolated cron sessions, even though the models were correctly listed in the bundled static catalog.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: The embedded runner's Attempt 2 (non-plugin-harness path) now resolves plugin-provided bundled static catalog models via `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v22.11.0, branch `fix/static-catalog-fallback-95500` based on origin/main
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
pnpm vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-embedded-agent.config.ts _proof_95500
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ pnpm vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-embedded-agent.config.ts _proof_95500 --reporter verbose

 RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdb/code/github/07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw

stdout | _proof_95500.test.ts > #95500 root cause verification > Attempt 1: skipAgentDiscovery → fail
  Attempt 1 (skipAgentDiscovery): ❌ Unknown model

stdout | _proof_95500.test.ts > #95500 root cause verification > Attempt 2 without allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback → fail
  Attempt 2 (no fallback):            ❌ Unknown model

stdout | _proof_95500.test.ts > #95500 root cause verification > Attempt 2 with allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback → success
  Attempt 2 (with fallback):           ✅ provider=opencode-go, id=deepseek-v4-flash

 ✓ |agents-embedded-agent| 3 passed
```

> **Note**: The above output is from a real local vitest run on Linux x86_64, Node v22.11.0, branch `fix/static-catalog-fallback-95500` based on origin/main. The proof-only test file was not committed — existing `model.test.ts` already covers `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback: true` in multiple scenarios (lines 664, 717, 821, 940), and a dedicated proof-only test would not add coverage.

**Matrix evidence**: Comparison of three resolution paths for opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash:

| Resolution path | allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Attempt 1: skipAgentDiscovery=true | not set | ❌ Unknown model |
| Attempt 2: full discovery | not set (pre-fix) | ❌ Unknown model |
| Attempt 2: full discovery (this fix) | true | ✅ provider=opencode-go, id=deepseek-v4-flash |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. When `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback: true` is set in Attempt 2's `resolveModelAsync` call, plugin-provided models in the bundled static catalog are correctly resolved
  2. Without this flag, Attempt 2 returns "Unknown model" for the same provider/model pair
  3. Attempt 1 behavior is unchanged (intentionally fails for non-plugin-harness)
  4. The fix is additive — no existing behavior is regressed

- **What was not tested**:
  - End-to-end cron session execution with a live plugin provider (requires plugin runtime)
  - Performance impact (single boolean flag addition to an existing options object)
  - Plugin-harness paths (pluginHarnessOwnsTransport already has its own fallback logic)

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The embedded runner's two-attempt model resolution in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts:961-982`:
1. Attempt 1 (line 935-957): `skipAgentDiscovery=true`, `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` only set when `pluginHarnessOwnsTransport=true`
2. If Attempt 1 fails, `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` is called (line 962)
3. Attempt 2 (line 965-982): `resolveModelAsync` called WITHOUT `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`

The bundled static catalog (`model.static-catalog.ts`) already contains the plugin provider's model entries (e.g., opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash). The only gap is that Attempt 2's `resolveModelAsync` call didn't enable the static catalog fallback path, so the static catalog was never queried.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — The change is additive (a single boolean flag). No existing tests assert that `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` should be false in this code path, so there is no regression risk.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Non-plugin-harness isolated cron sessions configured with a bundled static catalog plugin provider (e.g., opencode-go) will now correctly resolve the model instead of failing with "Unknown model". No other user-visible changes.

## Security Impact (required)

**All checkboxes must be checked or explained**

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: All three resolution paths (Attempt 1 without fallback, Attempt 2 without fallback, Attempt 2 with fallback) tested with opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash via focused vitest test
- Edge cases checked: Empty model registry (simulates missing models.json entries); no-op runtime hooks (isolates static catalog resolution from provider-runtime hooks)
- What you did NOT verify: Plugin-harness paths (separate fallback mechanism); configured fallback surface integration

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes (additive flag, no existing behavior changes)
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — The minimal change (5 lines, one boolean flag) corrects a deliberate omission in the Attempt 2 resolution path. The `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` option already existed and was already used by Attempt 1 for the plugin-harness case; this fix simply extends it to Attempt 2 for all harness types.
- **Refactor needed**: No — The two-attempt strategy is well-designed; the fix is a one-flag correction.
- **Alternative considered**: Could have modified `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` to merge plugin catalog entries into `models.json`, but that would be a larger, higher-risk change affecting the model persistence layer. The static catalog already has the correct model data; the issue is only that the resolution path doesn't query it.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: iCodeMate <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Full session containing issue analysis, code path tracing across 6+ files in the cron → embedded-agent-runner model resolution pipeline, root cause verification via vitest proof tests, and implementation

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk area**: If Attempt 2 already had a model resolved via discovery (not the static catalog), the `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` flag is never used — it only activates when `resolveModelAsync` fails its primary resolution paths. No risk of incorrect model selection.

**Mitigations**:
- The flag is only consulted when both discovery and dynamic resolution fail — it's a last-resort fallback
- The static catalog entries are curated and maintained by OpenClaw, not user-provided
- The same flag is already used in Attempt 1 for plugin-harness paths, so the behavior is well-tested

**Compatibility impact**: No breaking changes; existing configurations work without modification.

---

Fixes #95500

commands.author: chenyangjun-xy

target.title: fix(media): resolve absolute-path channel media for native vision blocks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram inbound images saved to disk are not passed to the model as native vision content blocks. The model receives only `<media:image>` placeholder text and cannot analyze the actual image. The same workflow works correctly through the webchat / Control UI.
- Why now: Any user sending images via Telegram DM to an OpenClaw bot with a vision-capable model cannot get the agent to see or describe the image. The image file is correctly downloaded to `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/` but is silently dropped during native vision block hydration.
- What changed:
  - `MediaAttachmentCache.resolveLocalPath()` now validates absolute filesystem paths against local roots via `resolveUsableLocalCandidate`, matching the existing relative-path validation added in 610691c796
  - `resolveCurrentTurnImages()` now returns successfully resolved images even when a subset fails, instead of discarding the entire batch
  - Added regression test for absolute-path media hydration
- What did NOT change: Relative-path handling is unchanged. Webchat inline-image path (early-return on `params.images`) is untouched. No new dependencies added.
- Outcome: Channel-delivered absolute-path images (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) are now hydrated into native vision content blocks instead of being silently dropped on a single read failure.
- Reviewer focus: `attachments.cache.ts` changes to `resolveLocalPath`, `current-turn-images.ts` changes to the all-or-nothing fallback logic, and the new test case.

## Linked context

Closes #93816 

Was this requested by a maintainer?
- No, but the issues have clear reproduction steps and root cause analysis.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `resolveLocalPath()` skipped absolute-path validation — it returned `path.resolve(rawPath)` directly while relative paths went through `resolveUsableLocalCandidate`. Channel-delivered inbound media (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) use absolute filesystem paths from `saveMediaBuffer`. When the path later failed `isInboundPathAllowed` in `ensureLocalStat`, the error was silently caught — channel attachments have `url` cleared, so `getBuffer` had no fallback and re-threw. Separately, `resolveCurrentTurnImages()` discarded all images when any single read failed.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test coverage for absolute-path media hydration. The existing test only covered relative paths. The symmetric relative-path validation in `resolveLocalPath` (added in 610691c796) was the guardrail that should have also covered absolute paths.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram inbound images saved to disk are not passed to the model as native vision content blocks; the model receives only `<media:image>` placeholder text and cannot analyze the actual image.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu 24.04 (x64), Node 22.22.0, OpenClaw main branch (9166165).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs output:
  ✓ hydrates Telegram-style state-relative media into native prompt images
  ✓ hydrates channel-delivered absolute-path media into native prompt images
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  2 passed (2)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The new absolute-path test passes alongside the existing relative-path test. resolveCurrentTurnImages now returns partially-resolved image batches instead of discarding all images on a single failure. The absolute-path validation in resolveLocalPath is symmetric with the existing relative-path handling.
- What was not tested: Not run against a live Telegram bot with a real image. The proof exercises the full hydration chain through the media store with local file I/O.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Unit-test-based — does not verify a live end-to-end Telegram roundtrip.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media-understanding/runner.attachments.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- Added 1 new test case:
  1. `hydrates channel-delivered absolute-path media into native prompt images` — writes a file under a temp state/media directory, sets `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and asserts absolute-path hydration into native `ImageContent` blocks

What failed before this fix, if known?
- Telegram inbound images appeared only as `<media:image>` placeholder text
- Any channel using `saveMediaBuffer` for inbound media could silently lose native vision blocks when a single attachment read failed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commands.author: pdurlej

target.title: feat(memory): carry source actor context into recall
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Memory recall can currently receive the conversation/session context without a stable indication of which group participant authored the triggering message. In multi-person channels, that can make retrieved or promoted memory read as if another participant's facts belong to the operator. This PR carries the generic source actor context into memory recall and prompt supplements so memory backends can preserve participant attribution without adding backend-specific logic.

## Summary

- Carries `SourceActor` from inbound context through embedded runs and plugin tool context.
- Exposes the same source actor context to memory corpus supplements for `memory_search` and `memory_get`.
- Adds memory prompt/flush guidance to preserve participant attribution instead of merging another participant's facts into the operator/user profile.

## Stack

This is stacked after #96738, `codex/signal-mention-facts-context`, and `codex/source-actor-context-contract`. Until those land, this PR's diff will include its dependencies.

## Production impact

No storage migration and no backend-specific behavior. This is not Honcho-specific: it adds a generic runtime/tool/memory contract that memory backends and supplements can consume. Core memory search still filters by existing session/source visibility; this PR does not claim a new per-person storage partition.

## Evidence

- Agent/tool context tests cover `SourceActor` propagation into plugin tool context.
- Auto-reply and inbound metadata tests cover carrying source actor context through reply runs.
- Memory-core tests cover source actor supplements in recall/citation paths and attribution guidance in memory output.
- Plugin SDK runtime mock tests cover `SourceActor*` parity with production inbound context.
- Broader affected-context regression suite passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- Focused SDK mock test passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- Production TypeScript check passed:
  `pnpm tsgo:prod`

### Redacted Runtime Proof

Temporary local proof harness imported production context/tool/memory code and executed `memory_search`/`memory_get` through a registered corpus supplement on PR head `7ad9265d3026`.

```console
$ node --import tsx proof-runtime-source-actor.mts
runtime-proof:environment
{
  "repo": "openclaw/openclaw",
  "head": "7ad9265d3026",
  "node": "v26.3.0"
}
runtime-proof:#96863 source actor reaches plugin tool context
{
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:signal:group:<redacted>",
  "sourceActor": {
    "id": "signal:<redacted-phone>",
    "peerId": "signal:<redacted-uuid>",
    "displayName": "Kasia",
    "role": "participant",
    "context": "signal-group:<redacted>"
  }
}
runtime-proof:#96864 source_actor received by memory_search consumer
{
  "received": [
    {
      "query": "Kasia family context",
      "agentSessionKey": "agent:main:signal:group:<redacted>",
      "sourceActor": {
        "id": "signal:<redacted-phone>",
        "peerId": "signal:<redacted-uuid>",
        "displayName": "Kasia",
        "role": "participant",
        "context": "signal-group:<redacted>"
      },
      "corpus": "wiki"
    }
  ]
}
runtime-proof:#96864 source_actor received by memory_get consumer
{
  "received": [
    {
      "lookup": "entities/kasia.md",
      "agentSessionKey": "agent:main:signal:group:<redacted>",
      "sourceActor": {
        "id": "signal:<redacted-phone>",
        "peerId": "signal:<redacted-uuid>",
        "displayName": "Kasia",
        "role": "participant",
        "context": "signal-group:<redacted>"
      },
      "corpus": "all"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`

target.title: feat(channels): expose source actor attribution
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Group-channel messages need a generic way to distinguish the runtime message author from the operator/user represented by the agent session. Without source actor attribution, downstream prompts, tools, and memory adapters can only see channel/session context and may conflate another group participant with the operator. This should be solved as a channel contract, not as a deployment-specific memory backend workaround.

## Summary

- Adds a generic `SourceActor` inbound context contract for the runtime identity of the message author.
- Maps Signal sender identity into `SourceActor` without making the contract Signal-specific.
- Documents that channel plugins should provide source actor attribution generically rather than hard-coding deployment-specific people or memory backends.

## Stack

This is stacked after #96738 and `codex/signal-mention-facts-context`. Until those land, this PR's diff will include its dependencies.

## Production impact

No behavior change by itself. The change gives prompts, tools, and memory integrations a stable way to distinguish a group participant from the operator/user.

## Evidence

- Channel context tests cover generic `SourceActor` projection.
- Signal mention-gating tests cover Signal sender identity mapping into the generic context.
- Plugin SDK runtime mock tests cover `SourceActor*` parity with production inbound context.
- Broader affected-context regression suite passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- Focused SDK mock test passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- Production TypeScript check passed:
  `pnpm tsgo:prod`

### Redacted Runtime Proof

Temporary local proof harness imported production `buildChannelInboundEventContext` and printed the emitted prompt context on PR head `5d2dc1faeae4`.

```console
$ node --import tsx proof-96863-source-actor.mts
runtime-proof:environment
{
  "pr": 96863,

  "node": "v26.3.0"

runtime-proof:#96863 source actor fields emitted by prompt context

  "SourceActor": {
    "id": "signal:<redacted-phone>",

  "SourceActorId": "signal:<redacted-phone>",
  "SourceActorPeerId": "signal:<redacted-uuid>",
  "SourceActorDisplayName": "Kasia",
  "SourceActorRole": "participant",
  "SourceActorContext": "signal-group:<redacted>"
}
```

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`

target.title: fix(discord): use configured statusReactions.timing instead of DEFAULT_TIMING
target.body:
## Summary

Makes the Discord message finalizer use user-configured `statusReactions.timing.doneHoldMs` / `errorHoldMs` instead of hardcoded `DEFAULT_TIMING` values, matching Telegram and Slack parity.

## What Problem This Solves

The Discord message finalizer has always ignored user-configured `statusReactions.timing.doneHoldMs` and `errorHoldMs`. After setting done/error emoji on the terminal status reaction, the finalizer sleeps `DEFAULT_TIMING.doneHoldMs` (1500ms) or `DEFAULT_TIMING.errorHoldMs` (2500ms) — regardless of what the user configured. Telegram and Slack both correctly use the configured values.

Closes #78431.

## Evidence

### Type 1: Standalone logic verification (zero dependencies)

The entire runtime fix is `{ ...DEFAULT_TIMING, ...cfg.messages?.statusReactions?.timing }` — a pure object spread with no side effects. A standalone proof script (no Vitest, no imports, runs with `node .proof-timing.mjs`) validates all cases:

```
$ node .proof-timing.mjs
✓ Test 1: Full config override — doneHoldMs=2000, errorHoldMs=4000
✓ Test 2: Partial config (doneHoldMs=3000) — errorHoldMs falls back to default (2500)
✓ Test 3: Empty config — both fall back to DEFAULT_TIMING
✓ Test 4a: Happy path (no error) -> resolvedTiming.doneHoldMs = 5000
✓ Test 4b: Error path (dispatchError=true) -> resolvedTiming.errorHoldMs = 10000
✓ Test 4c: finalDeliveryFailed -> resolvedTiming.errorHoldMs = 10000
✓ Test 5: Edge case — doneHoldMs=0 (immediate cleanup)
=== All 5 tests passed ===
```

### Type 2: Vitest regression tests (RED/GREEN cycle)

**Environment:** Windows 11, Node 24.16.0, pnpm 11.7.0, fake timers.

**RED** — reverted runtime fix to `DEFAULT_TIMING`, both strengthened tests fail because cleanup fires before the configured window:
```
 ❯ extension-discord  (109 tests | 2 failed | 107 skipped)
```

**GREEN** — re-applied fix, both tests pass:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed | 107 skipped (109)
   Start at  22:57:15
   Duration  36.70s
```

The strengthened tests now:
1. Advance to `DEFAULT_TIMING.doneHoldMs` (1500ms) — assert `removeReactionDiscord` was **not** called (fails on old default, passes with fix)
2. Advance remaining window to configured `2000ms` — assert cleanup **is** called
3. Matching `errorHoldMs` test: assert no removal at `2500ms`, then removal at configured `4000ms`

### Type 3: Sibling-channel parity (production-proven)

The same merge pattern `{ ...DEFAULT_TIMING, ...userTiming }` is already deployed in:
- **Slack** (`dispatch.ts:571`): sleeps `statusReactionTiming.doneHoldMs` / `errorHoldMs`
- **Telegram** (`bot-message-dispatch.ts`): same merge-before-sleep pattern

Discord was the only channel skipping this merge.

### Live Discord proof

Mantis command `@openclaw-mantis discord status reactions: verify configured doneHoldMs/errorHoldMs hold terminal Discord reactions before cleanup` has been posted. Awaiting Mantis run results.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The Discord message finalizer uses the configured `statusReactions.timing.doneHoldMs` / `errorHoldMs` instead of `DEFAULT_TIMING`. Terminal cleanup now waits for the configured hold, not the hardcoded 1500ms / 2500ms defaults.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout, Windows 11, Node 24.16.0, pnpm 11.7.0.

**Exact steps run after this patch:**

1. **RED — prove the strengthened regression catches the old default behavior.** Reverted the runtime fix in `message-handler.process.ts` (back to `DEFAULT_TIMING.doneHoldMs` / `errorHoldMs`), then ran the two configured-timing tests:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts -t "uses configured statusReactions.timing"
```

```
 ❯ extension-discord  ../../extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts (109 tests | 2 failed | 107 skipped) 3147ms
```

2. **GREEN — re-applied the fix and ran the two configured-timing tests:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts -t "uses configured statusReactions.timing"
```

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed | 107 skipped (109)
   Start at  22:57:15
   Duration  36.70s
```

**Observed result after fix:** Configured `doneHoldMs: 2000` is honored — the done emoji is held past the old 1500ms default and cleaned up only at 2000ms. Configured `errorHoldMs: 4000` is honored — the error emoji is held past the old 2500ms default and cleaned up only at 4000ms.

**What was not tested:** Live Discord guild. The three proof types above cover the fix from three independent angles: pure logic verification (standalone script), runtime behavior via fake-timer regression (Vitest), and production-proven sibling parity (Slack/Telegram). Live Discord would re-confirm the same `setTimeout` value selection already pinned by the unit tests — the bug is purely about which value the merge picks, and all three proofs converge on the same answer.

## Tests and validation

**Gate commands run locally:**

```bash
# Formatting
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts
# → All matched files use the correct format.

# Test type check (G17: *.test.ts changed)
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json \
  --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
# → exit 0, no type errors

# Standalone proof script (no framework dependency)
node .proof-timing.mjs
# → All 5 tests passed

# Targeted timing regression tests
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts -t "uses configured statusReactions.timing"
# → 2 passed, 107 skipped (109)
```

## Risk checklist

| Question | Yes/No |
| -------- | ------ |
| Is this a breaking change? | No |
| Does this add a new dependency? | No |
| Does this change user-facing config or API surface? | No |
| Does this affect other channels (Telegram, Slack)? | No |

Low risk. The runtime change is isolated to Discord's terminal sleep value selection. The compatibility note (existing non-default Discord `statusReactions.timing` configs now affect visible reaction lifetime) matches the documented contract and Telegram/Slack behavior.
